input: What is the course code and credits for Digital Electronics and Logic Design?
List the units and topics covered in EC-211 Digital Electronics and Logic Design.

units.topic: Introduction: Number systems, Binary Arithmetic, BCD codes, Excess-3 code, Gray code, Hamming code, DAC, ADC
Logic Gates and Logic Families: RTL, DTL, TTL, ECL, MOS and CMOS
Combinational Logic Design: Boolean Algebra, Karnaugh-Map, Tabulation method, VEM method
MSI and PLD Components: Binary Adder/Subtractor, Decoders, Encoders, Multiplexers, PROM, PLA, PAL
Sequential Logic Design: Latches, Flip-flops, Registers, Counters, Finite State Machine

input: What are the course outcomes for Analog Electronics EC-212?

course_outcomes: CO1: Develop the ability to analyze and design analog electronic circuits using discrete components.
CO2: Understand the use of small-signal models to predict gain and behavior in transistor amplifier.
CO3: Describe the design trade-offs in analog amplifier circuits.
CO4: Design tuned amplifiers and apply them in a communications system.

input: What subjects are taught in the 3rd semester of B.Tech ECE at NIT Hamirpur?

subjects.name: Digital Electronics and Logic Design Lab

input: What subjects are taught in the 4th semester of B.Tech ECE at NIT Hamirpur?
What are the Discipline Elective-I options in the 4th semester?
Describe the syllabus for VLSI Technology EC-214.

units.topic: Introduction to VLSI: Miniaturization of Electronic Systems
Monolithic Fabrication Techniques: Crystal growth, Epitaxial Process, Oxidation, Lithography, Etching, Diffusion and Ion Implantation, Metallization
Packaging of VLSI Chip: packaging process, package design considerations, package types
Isolation Techniques in Monolithic Components: Diodes, BJT and MOSFETs
Monolithic Components Prototype Fabrication: Diodes, npn BJT, pnp BJT, MOSFETs, n-MOS, p-MOS, CMOS, Resistors and Capacitors

books: VLSI Technology by S.M. Sze, Tata Mc-Graw Hill
VLSI Fabrication Principles by S.K. Gandhi, John Willey & Sons
Integrated Circuits by K.R. Botkar, Khanna Publishers

What subjects are available as Open Electives in ECE B.Tech?
What are the subjects in the 6th semester of B.Tech ECE?

subjects.name: Communication Systems and Network Simulation Lab

What are the Stream Core-II and Stream Core-III subjects?
What subjects are taught in the 8th semester of B.Tech ECE?

Embedded Networking and Device Driver Programming

input: What are the Stream Elective-II options in the 8th semester?

Nanophotonics and Plasmonics: Concept and Applications

input: What is the total credit distribution in the B.Tech ECE program at NIT Hamirpur?
What are the course objectives of EC-312 Digital Communication and Systems?

course_objectives: To impart knowledge about the key modules of digital communication systems with emphasis on digital modulation techniques.
To introduce the fundamental concepts relevant to reception of digital signals.
To enable the students to understand the concept and basics of information theory and the basics of channel coding/decoding.

input: What are the units in EC-313 Control Systems?

units.topic: Basic Concepts: Historical Review, Definitions, Classification, Open and Closed Loop Systems
Mathematical Models of Control System: Transfer Function, Electrical, Mechanical and Thermal Systems, Block Diagrams and Signal Flow Graphs
Control Components: DC Servomotor, AC Servomotor, Potentiometers, Stepper Motor
Time and Frequency Domain Analysis: Transient and Frequency Response, P, PD, PI and PID Controllers
Stability Analysis: Routh-Hurwitz Criterion, Root Locus, Nyquist Criterion, Bode Plots
State Variable Analysis: State Space Representation, Controllability and Observability

input: What are the reference books for EC-322 Antenna and Wave Propagation?

books_and_references: Antennas Theory by C.A. Balanis, Willey Publication.
Antennas by J.D. Kraus, McGraw Hill.
Antennas and Radio Propagation by R.E. Collins, McGraw-Hill.
Electromagnetic waves & radiating System, E.C. Jorden and B.C. Balmann, P.H.I.

input: What experiments are conducted in the Digital Electronics and Logic Design Lab EC-216?

output.course_name: Digital Electronics and Logic Design Lab
experiments: 1. Study about logic gates and verify their truth tables.
2. Realization of AND and OR gates using diodes/resistors and universal gates.
3. Design and implement half adder and full adder circuits.
4. Design and implement half subtractor and full subtractor circuits.
5. Design and implement 4-bit binary to gray code converter.
6. Design and implement BCD to excess-3 code converter.
7. Design and implement 2-bit and 8-bit magnitude comparators.
8. Design and implement multiplexer and demultiplexer.
9. Implementation of Boolean functions using multiplexer.
10. Design and implement encoder and decoder.
11. Realization of SR, JK, D and T flip flops using gates.
12. Design and implement 3-bit synchronous up counter.
13. Design and implement 3-bit asynchronous up/down counter.
14. Design BCD to seven segment display using IC 7447.

input: What are the course outcomes for EC-321 Digital Signal Processing?

course_outcomes: CO1: Understand the discrete time signals and systems.
CO2: Understand the Fourier transform and Fourier series of discrete time signals.
CO3: Analyse the discrete time signals in frequency domain using DFT and FFT.
CO4: Understand the Z-Transform and its properties.
CO5: Understand the realization structures for FIR and IIR digital filters.
CO6: Analyse the design characteristic of FIR and IIR filters.

input: What is the syllabus of EC-411 Computer Architecture and Organization?

units.topic: Basics of Computer Architecture: Codes, Number System, Logic gates, Flip flops, Registers, Counters, Multiplexer, Bus & memory transfer, Logic micro operations
Basic Computer Organization: Instruction codes, Timing & control, Instruction Cycles, Memory reference instruction, I/O and Interrupts
ARM Processor Fundamentals: ARM core data flow model, Architecture, General Purpose Register set, Exceptions, Interrupts, ARM processors family
Central Processing Unit: General register organization, RISC, CISC, Multiplication Algorithms, Division algorithms, DMA transfer, I/O processor
Memory Unit: Memory hierarchy, Cache memory, Associative memory, Virtual memory, Memory management
Introduction to Parallel Processing: Pipelining, Multiprocessors, Interconnection structures

input: What are the course objectives and outcomes for EC-432 Neural Network and Deep Learning?

course_objectives: To develop artificial intelligence and machine learning techniques for solving various real-life problems.
course_outcomes: CO1: Understand the fundamentals of artificial intelligence and machine learning.
CO2: Understand the concepts of selected machine learning algorithms.
CO3: Understand the concepts of neural network learning.
CO4: Understand the concepts of deep neural networks.

input: What topics are covered in EC-432 Neural Network and Deep Learning?

units.topic: Neural Network Learning: Models of a Neuron, MLP, Back Propagation, Loss Functions, Gradient Descent, MLP for Classification and Regression
Deep Learning: Unsupervised Learning, Autoencoders, CNN, Transfer Learning, RNN, Dropout, Batch Normalization
Effective training in Deep Net: early stopping, Dropout, Batch Normalization, Instance Normalization, Group Normalization
Recent Trends: GAN, CNN and RNN for Signal Classification, Image Segmentation, Image Denoising, Object Detection
Classical Supervised Tasks, Image Denoising, Semantic Segmentation, LSTM Networks, Variational Autoencoder, GAN

input: What is the syllabus for EC-465 Internet of Things and Applications?

units.topic: Introduction to IoT: Sensing, Actuation, IoT Networking, Connectivity Technologies, Communication Protocols, Sensor Networks, UAV Networks, M2M Communications
Interoperability in IoT, Arduino Programming, Sensors and Actuators with Arduino, Python programming, Raspberry Pi
Implementation of IoT with Raspberry Pi, SDN for IoT, Data Handling and Analytics
Cloud Computing: Fundamentals, Service Models, Service Management, Sensor-Cloud, Fog Computing
Applications: Smart Cities, Smart Homes, Connected Vehicles, Smart Grid, Industrial IoT, Agriculture, Healthcare, Activity Monitoring

input: Describe the course EC-224 Electromagnetic Field Theory.

units.topic: Introduction: Vector algebra, Coordinate systems (rectangular, cylindrical, spherical), Gradient, Divergence, Curl
Electrostatics: Coulomb's law, Gauss's law, Electric flux density, Laplace's and Poisson's equations
Magnetostatics: Biot-Savart's law, Ampere's Circuital law, Lorentz force equation, Magnetic Vector Potential
Time Varying EM Field: Maxwell's equations, Plane wave in dielectric and conductor, Poynting theorem
Transmission Line: Model, Reflections, VSWR, Impedance matching, Quarter and half wave lines, stub matching

input: What programming experiments are done in EC-227 Microprocessors and Microcontrollers Lab?

experiments: 1. On 8085 kit, find the Factorial of a number.
2. On 8085 kit, find if a number is prime or a perfect square.
3. On 8051 kit, write a program to perform serial data transfer.
4. On 8051 kit, generate square wave for a given frequency and duty cycle.
5. On cortex M3, write a program to perform LED blinking.
6. On cortex M3, write a program to verify Digitalout.
7. On cortex M3, write a program to display clock on 7-segment display.
8. On cortex M3, write a program to generate Analog output.
9. On cortex M3, write a program to read in Analog input.
10. On cortex M3, write a program to debug using serial pc.
11. On cortex M3, write a program to generate PWM output.
12. On cortex M3, write a program to perform counting on LCD counter.
13. On cortex M3, write a program to learn Interrupt function.
14. On cortex M3, write a program to understand I2C master and slave communication.
15. On Intel Galileo Gen 2, plot a graph for analog input.
16. On Intel Galileo Gen 2, write an Array in Arduino.

input: What are the objectives and outcomes of EC-463 Low Power VLSI Design Techniques?

course_objectives: To impart knowledge about the dominant sources of power dissipation in VLSI circuits.
To introduce fundamental concepts for optimization of power at all design levels: technology, circuit, logic, and architectural level.
To enable the students to be aware of power estimation by various means.
course_outcomes: CO1: Identify sources of power dissipation in VLSI systems.
CO2: Analyze various circuit and logic design techniques for dynamic and leakage power reduction.
CO3: Design arithmetic circuits, latches and flip flops with different logic styles.
CO4: Understand the concepts of probability and random logic signals for estimation of capacitance and power dissipation.
CO5: Understand power management through architectural level techniques.

input: What is the complete list of experiments in EC-325 Antenna and Microwave Systems Lab?

experiments: 1. Study of Microwave Components, Sources and Different Types of Loads at X Band Setup.
2. Study of Characteristics of Reflex Klystron Oscillator and its Mechanical and Electronics Tuning Range.
3. Study of V-I Characteristics of Gunn Diode and its Negative Resistance.
4. Determine Insertion Loss and Isolation Parameter of a Ferrite Based Isolator and Circulators.
5. Measure Frequency in a Rectangular Waveguide and Relationship among Frequency, Free Space Wavelength and Guide Wavelength.
6. Study Characteristics of Various Tees: E-Plane Tee, H-Plane Tee and Magic Tees.
7. Measure Coupling and Directivity of a 3 dB, 10 dB and 20 dB Directional Couplers.
8. Measure Low, Medium, and High VSWR of DUT Using Slotted Lines Section.
9. Measure Unknown Impedances using Smith Chart.
10. Measure VSWR, Insertion Loss, Attenuation of Fixed and Variable Attenuators.
11. Plot Radiation Pattern of a Pyramidal Horn Antenna and determine Gain and Beam width.
12. Study of Vector Network Analyzer (VNA) and its applications.
13. Study the E-and H-field of a Rectangular Waveguide using HFSS/CST.

input: What are the reference books for EC-412 Wireless Communication?

books_and_references: Wireless Communications: Principles and Practice by Theodore S. Rappaport, Pearson / PHI Publications.
Wireless Communications and Networks: 3G and Beyond by Iti Saha Misra, Tata McGraw Hill Publications.
Mobile Cellular Telecommunications: Analog and Digital Systems by William C.Y. Lee, Tata McGraw Hill Publication.
Wireless Digital Communications by Kamilo Feher, PHI Publication.

input: Describe the course structure and syllabus of MA-218 Numerical Analysis and Statistics.

units.topic: Introduction to Complex Numbers: De Moivre's theorem, Exponential, Circular, Hyperbolic and Logarithmic functions
Functions of Complex Variable: Cauchy-Riemann equations, Analytic functions, Complex integration, Taylor and Laurent series, Cauchy's residue theorem
Interpolation: Least square curve fit, Finite differences, Newton's interpolation formulae, Lagrange's interpolation
Numerical Integration and Differentiation: Trapezoidal, Simpson's rules, Taylor series, Picard's, Euler's, Runge-Kutta methods
Numerical Solution of Linear and Non Linear Equations: Bisection, Regula Falsi, Newton-Raphson, Jacobi and Gauss-Seidel methods
Probability and Statistics: Conditional probability, Binomial, Poisson and Normal distributions

input: What is the total number of credits in each course type for the ECE B.Tech program?
What are the course objectives for EC-341 Data Communication and Computer Networks?

course_objectives: To introduce basic concepts of Data communication with different models including the OSI and TCP/IP reference models.
Enumerate the physical layer, Data Link Layer, Network Layer, Transport Layer and Application Layer and explain the function of each layer.
Understanding of switching concept and different types of switching techniques.

input: List the experiments for EC-324 Digital Signal Processing Lab.

experiments: 1. Demonstrate the Nyquist-Shannon sampling theorem and perform sampling and reconstruction of analog signals.
2. Generate and analyze fundamental signals (sinusoidal, square, triangular, sawtooth, noise) using Fourier transform.
3. Perform linear convolution, correlation and circular convolution using MATLAB.
4. Perform Z-transform of signals using MATLAB and analyse properties of Z-transform.
5. Perform DFT of signals using MATLAB and analyze frequency domain characteristics.
6. Implement FFT algorithm to reduce computation complexity in MATLAB.
7. Design and implement digital filters: low-pass, high-pass, band-pass, and band-stop using FIR and IIR structures.
8. Image Processing: perform image filtering, edge detection, and image compression using MATLAB.
9. DSP Hardware Implementation on TMS320C6713 Processor Kits.
10. Project Work on a specific DSP application (audio processing, speech recognition, or image processing).

input: What is the syllabus for EC-471 Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning?

units.topic: Intelligent Agents and Problem-Solving: Foundations of AI, History, Rational Agents, Search Algorithms, Heuristic Functions
Introduction to Machine Learning: Bayesian Decision Theory, PCA, Linear Discriminant Analysis
Selected ML Algorithms: Linear Regression, Decision Tree, SVM, Logistic Regression, Kernels, HMM
Ensemble Learning and Clustering: Boosting, K-means, Fuzzy K-means, Hierarchical Agglomerative Clustering

input: What are the reference books for EC-314 VLSI Design?

books_and_references: CMOS Digital Integrated Circuits-Analysis & Design by S.M. Kang and Y. Leblebici, TMH.
Design of Analog CMOS Integrated Circuits by B. Razavi, TMH.
Solid State Electronic Devices by B.G. Streetman and S. Banerjee, PHI.
Principles of CMOS VLSI Design-A Systems Perspective by Neil H.E. Weste and K. Eshraghian.
Introduction to VLSI by K. Eshraghian and Pucknell, PHI.

input: What are the course outcomes for EC-415 Communication Systems and Network Simulation Lab?

output.course_name: Communication Systems and Network Simulation Lab
course_outcomes: CO1: Understand the fundamental concepts of communication systems and networks, including signal processing, coding, modulation, and multiple access techniques.
CO2: Analyse and evaluate the performance of digital communication systems using simulation and modelling techniques.
CO3: Proficiently use simulation tools and software (MATLAB/Simulink, Python, Network simulators) to design, implement and evaluate communication systems.
CO4: Develop critical thinking skills, problem-solving skills, and apply theoretical concepts to practical scenarios.
CO5: Prepare for further study or research in the field of communication systems and networks.

input: What is the course EC-451 Information Theory and Coding about?

units.topic: Measures of Information and Channel Capacity: Entropy, Mutual Information, Jensen Inequality, Channel Capacity for BNC, BSC, BEC
Data Compression: Unique Decodability, Kraft's Inequality, Huffman, Shannon-Fano-Elias, Lempel-Ziv coding
Design of Linear Block Codes: Syndrome, Error Detection, Minimum Distance, Encoder and Syndrome Decoder
Convolutional Codes: Encoding, Structural Properties, Distance Properties, Encoder and Decoder design

input: What is EC-498 Holistic Assessment?
What are the books for EC-213 Communication Theory?

books_and_references: Elements of Communication Theory by J.C. Hancock, McGraw-Hill Education Publisher.
Principles of Communication System by Taub & Schilling, McGraw-Hill Education Publisher.
Communication Systems by S. Haykin, Wiley Publication.

input: What is the structure of EC-382 Simulation of Communication Systems?

units.topic: Introduction to Communication Systems and Programming Essentials: Signal representation, Analog/Digital modulation, MATLAB and Python basics
Communication System Modeling in MATLAB: Modulating, Channel modeling, Analog and Digital modulation simulation, Performance analysis
Communication System Modeling in Python: NumPy, SciPy, Matplotlib, OOP for complex system modeling, Advanced signal processing
Advanced Topics: Channel coding and decoding, Synchronization methods, MIMO systems, Software-defined radio

input: What are the course objectives for EC-481 Adaptive Signal Processing?

course_objectives: To introduce signal processing techniques for adaptive systems.
To understand the applications of adaptive systems in communications, radar, sonar, seismology, navigation systems and biomedical engineering.
To study the basic principles of adaptation, various adaptive signal processing algorithms for adaptive noise cancellation, interference cancelling, and system identification.

input: What is the syllabus for HS-321 Engineering Economics and Accountancy?

units.topic: Introduction to Engineering Economics: Theory of Demand & Supply, Law of Demand, Elasticity, Demand Forecasting
Production and Cost: Production functions, Laws of Returns to Scale, Cost curves, Break even analysis
Costing and Appraisal: Cost elements, Overhead cost, Criteria of project appraisal, Social cost benefit analysis
Markets: Perfect Competition, Monopoly, Monopolistic Competition, Oligopoly, Price and Output Determination
Money: Monetary Policy, Fiscal Policy, Banking, Central Bank, CRR, Bank Rate, Repo Rate, SLR
Depreciation: Straight line, Diminishing balance, Annuity and Sinking Fund methods
Financial Accounting: Double entry system, Journal, Ledger, Trial Balance, Trading Account, Profit and Loss Account, Balance Sheet

input: Which semester does Industrial Training Evaluation EC-416 happen and what are its details?

output.note: Students should undergo vocational training during summer vacations after the sixth semester.

input: What is the UG Project EC-499 and what credit does it carry?

output.note: Students opting for internship will complete the UG project. The remaining credit requirements will be fulfilled by opting Free Elective Courses.
